Why Does Child Care Seem Less Affordable Than Ever?
Child care is always a considerable expense for parents. But prices are rising faster than inflation, and it’s one of the main factors behind Americans’ concerns about affordability.
One reason prices have gone up is that child care providers’ own expenses have increased — for food, supplies, insurance and especially for labor, their biggest expenditure.
Child care providers operate on such slim margins that they have little choice but to pass on higher costs to parents, or decrease their take-home pay.
At the same time, government support for the child care industry is shrinking. Pandemic aid has ended, and many states have cut funding that helps low-income families pay for care.
Yet there’s generally agreement that the child care affordability crisis is likely to get worse without some form of government support, whether it’s subsidized child care or larger tax credits.
